title: I searched, but wasn't sure I found

messages.author: NoahK
messages.text: As long as they were at the same voltage when put in parallel, and they are the same model of cell with a similar number of cycles, then there shouldn't be any issues using an already working charging circuit. There is no balancer for cells in parallel: them being in parallel ensures they are balanced by nature. (edited)
messages.content_html: As long as they were at the same voltage when put in parallel, and they are the same model of cell with a similar number of cycles, then there shouldn't be any issues using an already working charging circuit. There is no balancer for cells in parallel: them being in parallel ensures they are balanced by nature. (edited)

messages.author: NoahK
messages.text: I guess the real question is, what is the "odd battery behavior" that you are experiencing?
messages.content_html: I guess the real question is, what is the "odd battery behavior" that you are experiencing?
messages.reply_html:
Kuma 🐻
A friend and I set ours up like that, with them in parallel, and we're having some odd battery behavior. Probably just due to them being cheap… but was wondering if there's some clever balance circuit we should be putting in instead of just the Heltec v4's built-in charging circuit

messages.author: Kuma 🐻
messages.text: Oh, just running it with a heltec v4, and it's never getting up to 4.2, and seems to be dying at 3.5, though I feel like that should be enough to keep powering a 3.3 board like the heltec?
messages.content_html: Oh, just running it with a heltec v4, and it's never getting up to 4.2, and seems to be dying at 3.5, though I feel like that should be enough to keep powering a 3.3 board like the heltec?
messages.reply_html:
NoahK
I guess the real question is, what is the "odd battery behavior" that you are experiencing?

messages.author: Kuma 🐻
messages.text: Thanks for the patient explanation, I thought that's how a parallel pack would work (esp with same brand, voltage to start like you said) but think I got myself confused
messages.content_html: Thanks for the patient explanation, I thought that's how a parallel pack would work (esp with same brand, voltage to start like you said) but think I got myself confused
messages.reply_html:
NoahK
If they were at different voltages, when connected in parallel, there may have been a large in rush current from the higher voltage to the lower voltage cell. This might have damaged one or both of the cells, depending on their charge differential and current capacities. Being different models of cell may cause them to charge and discharge it different rates, but with how slow the charge and discharge rate is from the Heltec's built-in charge controller, this is a non-issue. They will continuously equalize their voltages just by nature of being connected in parallel.

messages.author: NoahK
messages.text: 3.5 minus the drop-out of the LDO would be about 3.3
messages.content_html: 3.5 minus the drop-out of the LDO would be about 3.3

messages.text: Ahh, okay. Thanks! I do a lot of software, but EE always seems like dark magic, high school physics is rattling around back there somewhere but never got to some of this stuff I thought I saw in a recent commit that there was some options in the new hardware to manually set battery charge estimates? Do you know anything about that?

3.5 minus the drop-out of the LDO would be about 3.3
